description_long: This house features a prominent red-tiled hip roof with intersecting gables, giving it a classic suburban appearance. The exterior walls are finished in light gray, complemented by green-framed windows and a wooden front door. The front facade includes a covered porch supported by two columns, providing a sheltered entryway. Windows are symmetrically arranged on the front and side elevations, while the rear and one side have minimal fenestration. The structure appears to be a single-story dwelling with a spacious footprint, suitable for a residential neighborhood. The overall design suggests a blend of traditional and contemporary architectural elements.
description: A single-story house with a red-tiled hip roof, light gray walls, green windows, and a covered front porch.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with a covered porch, green windows, and the prominent gabled roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the large expanse of the hip roof and the depth of the house, with minimal window openings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain wall with no visible windows or doors, dominated by the roof structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing a single green window and the intersection of the roof sections.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows on front and side, minimal on rear
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with two columns, overhanging gable roof, single wooden door, and steps leading to entry
